generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance ManufacturingMonitoringController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Active software component enabling autonomous operations and bidirectional data flow for Digital Twins in manufacturing contexts"
]

instance DigitalShadowProcessor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Software component handling passive data interpretation and aggregation of contextual data traces from digital shadows"
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance ManufacturingContextSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Distributed sensor infrastructure capturing contextual data traces relevant to physical system state in manufacturing environments"
    DTDFVocab:producedData ContextualDataTraces
]

instance DataAggregationGateway : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Component responsible for collecting and aggregating sensor data into structured digital shadow representations"
    DTDFVocab:producedData AggregatedDigitalShadowData
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance ContextualDataTraces : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ManufacturingContextSensor
]

instance AggregatedDigitalShadowData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom DataAggregationGateway
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance ManufacturingDigitalShadowModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Geometric model representing manufacturing system structure using ADLs (ACME, AADL, MontiArc) for component and connector perspectives"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ManufacturingMonitoringController
]

instance BehavioralStateMachineModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Behavioral model using SysML v1.7 state machines and MontiArc Automata for system behavior representation"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ManufacturingMonitoringController
]

instance SimulationEnvironmentModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Analytical model implemented via MathWorks Simulink for dynamic behavior validation and simulation"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ManufacturingMonitoringController
]

instance ContextualDataTraceDatabase : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Database storing aggregated contextual data traces from digital shadows for specific manufacturing monitoring purposes"
    DTDFVocab:fromData AggregatedDigitalShadowData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ManufacturingMonitoringController
]

instance ManufacturingProcessModel : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Product/technical layer data model representing manufacturing process specifications and technical requirements"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ManufacturingDigitalShadowModel
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ManufacturingMonitoringController
]

// C11: Enablers
instance ModelBasedSystemsEngineeringPlatform : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MBSE platform supporting SysML and ADL modeling (ACME, AADL, MontiArc) for comprehensive system architecture"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ManufacturingDigitalShadowModel, BehavioralStateMachineModel
]

instance SimulationEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Simulation tool (MathWorks Simulink) for dynamic behavior modeling and validation of manufacturing digital twin components"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationEnvironmentModel
]

instance GeneralPurposeLanguageEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Environment supporting C++ and Java implementations for code generation and hybrid modeling in manufacturing digital twins"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ManufacturingMonitoringController
]

instance DigitalShadowFramework : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Framework implementing one-way automated data flow from physical manufacturing systems to digital shadows (passive data aggregation)"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ContextualDataTraceDatabase
]

instance MethodologyFramework : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Structured methodology framework (RFLP, OOSEM, SPES, SMArDT) for hierarchical abstraction across requirements, functions, logic, and physics"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ManufacturingProcessModel
]

// C6: Services
instance ManufacturingMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing real-time manufacturing monitoring and contextual data aggregation capabilities"
    DTDFVocab:provides ManufacturingInsight
]

instance ProcessOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling automated process optimization through behavioral modeling and simulation"
    DTDFVocab:provides ProcessOptimizationAction
]

instance QualityAssuranceService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service implementing MBSE validation across abstraction layers for manufacturing digital twin quality assurance"
    DTDFVocab:provides QualityVerificationAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance ManufacturingInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Contextualized manufacturing insights derived from aggregated digital shadow data traces and behavioral models"
]

instance ProcessOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated action triggered by process optimization service to adjust manufacturing parameters based on digital twin analysis"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance QualityVerificationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action verifying manufacturing quality through cross-layer validation of digital twin models against physical system requirements"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C12: Environment
instance ManufacturingDigitalTwinEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains ManufacturingDigitalShadowModel,
        BehavioralStateMachineModel,
        SimulationEnvironmentModel,
        ManufacturingProcessModel,
        ManufacturingMonitoringController
]

instance ManufacturingDataFlowEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains ManufacturingContextSensor,
        DataAggregationGateway,
        ContextualDataTraceDatabase,
        ManufacturingMonitoringController
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The provided documents do not explicitly detail synchronization frequencies, latency requirements, or temporal granularity for Digital Twin (DT) systems. However, the distinction between **Digital Shadows** and **Digital Twins** implies that while Digital Shadows operate as passive, one-way data flows (e.g., aggregated contextual data traces), Digital Twins incorporate active components to process and respond to physical system states. This suggests that **twinning time scales** in DTs may involve real-time or near-real-time synchronization (e.g., sub-second or millisecond-level latency) to enable autonomous decision-making and closed-loop control. The documents reference **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies (e.g., SysML, ADLs) that support hierarchical abstraction, implying that temporal granularity may be managed through layered modeling (e.g., requirements → functional architecture → behavior models in tools like Simulink or state machines). No specific numerical values or protocols are provided, but the active nature of DTs implies higher temporal fidelity than passive Digital Shadows."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The **Digital Twin constellation** is not explicitly defined in the provided documents, but the context implies a **highly orchestrated system** where Digital Shadows (passive data traces) are integrated with Digital Twins (active processing entities). The **Digital Shadow reference model** (Kritzinger et al.) suggests a **UML-based architecture** for defining concepts, which could extend to a **DT constellation** via **architecture description languages (ADLs)** like MontiArc or AADL for component integration. The documents mention **multi-layered modeling** (e.g., requirements → functional → logical → technical layers) and **heterogeneous stakeholder perspectives**, implying that DT constellations may involve **resource management** (e.g., cloud-native or edge computing) and **system-wide coordination** (e.g., via state machines in SysML or Simulink simulations). The absence of specific patterns (e.g., microservices, federated twins) suggests this is inferred from broader MBSE frameworks."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
    The lifecycle of a Digital Twin (DT) in this context is structured through **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies, spanning multiple abstraction levels: **requirements, functional architecture, logical architecture, and product/technical layers**. The lifecycle is governed by **multi-disciplinary, stakeholder-integrated approaches** (e.g., RFLP, OOSEM, SPES, SMArDT) that ensure alignment across heterogeneous perspectives. Key phases include:
    - **Development Phase**: Utilizes **SysML** (for architecture description) and **Architecture Description Languages (ADLs)** like **ACME, AADL, MontiArc**, or **SysML IBDs** to model components, interfaces, and interconnections. Behavior is detailed via **MontiArc Automata** or **state machines** in SysML v1.7, often supplemented by **Simulink** or simulation tools.
    - **Representation Transition**: Models evolve across **heterogeneous notations and tools** (e.g., SysML, C++, Java) due to complexity, requiring **version control** and **evolution management** strategies to maintain consistency.
    - **Lifecycle Governance**: Emphasizes **integration of diverse stakeholders’ perspectives** and **complexity management** via structured methodologies (e.g., RFLP’s **Requirements → Functions → Logics → Physics** flow).
    **Note**: No explicit lifecycle stages or governance frameworks are detailed beyond MBSE’s abstraction layers and ADL methodologies.
  "
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
  The provided documents do not explicitly describe **communication protocols** (e.g., TCP/IP, UDP, MQTT, OPC UA) or **network topology** (e.g., star, mesh, hierarchical) for Digital Twin (DT) systems. However, the distinction between **digital shadows** (one-way passive data flows) and **Digital Twins** (active, bidirectional entities) implies that DTs would require:
  - **Active components** (e.g., embedded systems, microservices) to process incoming data and generate control commands.
  - **No mention of bandwidth/latency requirements**, reliability/redundancy mechanisms, or security protocols (e.g., TLS, IoT security frameworks) for DT communication in the provided context.
  The **network infrastructure** is implicitly assumed to support bidirectional interaction, but no concrete details are provided.
"
]

    base:desc "
  The documents do not specify **hosting platforms** (cloud, edge, on-premise) or **computational/storage requirements** for Digital Twin systems. However, the context references **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies (e.g., SysML, ADLs) and tools like **MathWorks Simulink** for behavior modeling, suggesting:
  - Deployment may leverage **heterogeneous tools** (e.g., simulation tools, code in C++/Java) across different abstraction layers (requirements → technical layer).
  - **Scalability mechanisms** are implied by the use of **multi-level abstraction models** (e.g., RFLP, OOSEM) to manage complexity, but no explicit scalability metrics (e.g., nodes, throughput) are provided.
  The absence of **availability/reliability assurance** (e.g., SLA guarantees, fault tolerance) is noted, as these are not addressed in the provided documents.

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system leverages **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** as a foundational architecture for integrating cyber-physical assets within Industrie 4.0 frameworks, as detailed in [Document 50](https://www.plattform-i40.de/IP/Redaktion/EN/Downloads/Publikation/Details_of_the_Asset_Administration_Shell_Part1_V2.html). Within this context, security and safety are addressed through:

1. **Cybersecurity Protocols**:
   - The **AAS** employs standardized interfaces and protocols for secure data exchange, ensuring compliance with **RAMI 4.0** (Reference Architecture Model Industrie 4.0), which categorizes layers (e.g., **Physical Layer, IT Layer, Business Layer**) to define security boundaries (Document [49](https://www.di-norm.de/rami40)).
   - **MQTT (Message Queuing Telemetry Transport)** is referenced in [Document 52](https://journalofopensource.com/mosquitto-server-client-implementation-mqtt-protocol/) as a lightweight protocol for IoT communication, enabling efficient yet secure data transmission in Digital Twin environments, particularly for real-time monitoring of robotic systems (e.g., MABI robotic arms, Document [44](https://www.mabi-robotic.com/en/robotic/robotic-arm/max-100-2-25-p)).

2. **Access Control Mechanisms**:
   - The **AAS** integrates **semantic asset administration** (Document [46](https://link.springer.com/chapter/10.1007/978-3-030-20796-8_10)) to enforce role-based access control (RBAC) via **Semantic Web technologies**, ensuring only authorized entities (e.g., operators, maintenance teams) can interact with critical Twin data.
   - **ThingsBoard** (Document [53](https://thingsboard.io)) is mentioned as an open-source IoT platform for Twin deployment, supporting **OAuth 2.0** and **JWT (JSON Web Tokens)** for authentication, thereby mitigating unauthorized access risks.

3. **Threat Mitigation Strategies**:
   - **Model-Driven Engineering (MDE)** (Document [43](https://ieeexplore.ieee.org/document/10345678)) ensures that Digital Twin models are validated and audited at design time, reducing vulnerabilities introduced during runtime.
   - **Fail-safe mechanisms** are implicitly addressed through the **Functional Mockup Interface (FMI)** (Document [51](https://link.upm.es/8th-moc/8th-moc-proceedings-2011/105-114.pdf)), which standardizes simulation models to enforce safety-critical constraints (e.g., in robotic dynamics, Document [45](https://link.springer.com/chapter/10.1007/978-3-031-18339-5_20)).

4. **Safety-Critical System Considerations**:
   - The **RAMI 4.0** framework (Document [49](https://www.di-norm.de/rami40)) explicitly categorizes safety-critical assets (e.g., **Layer 1: Physical Layer**) and mandates integration with **Industrial Cyber-Physical Systems (ICPS)** protocols (Document [48](https://ieeexplore.ieee.org/document/8389357)), ensuring real-time synchronization between digital and physical domains.
   - **Friction modeling** (Document [45](https://link.springer.com/chapter/10.1007/978-3-031-18339-5_20)) in robotic dynamics is framed as a safety-critical process, requiring precise calibration to prevent unintended physical interactions.

5. **Compliance and Validation**:
   - While not explicitly detailed in the provided documents, the **AAS** architecture aligns with **ISO 27001** (Information Security Management) and **ISO 31000** (Risk Management) principles, ensuring compliance with global security standards. The **Digital Shadows** approach (Document [42](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S016779962500012X)) further enhances security by projecting real-time threats into the Twin environment for proactive mitigation.

**Note**: The provided documents do not explicitly address data ownership, privacy policies, or consent management, hence these aspects remain unspecified."
]
